---
id: "goat-climb-dune"
created: "2026-03"
attribution: QRI-2026
use: "Chapter 12 – rates of change, marginal rate"
---

```{r include=FALSE, eval=!"devoirs" %in% (.packages())}
library(devoirs)
library(mosaicCalc)
```

::: {#exr-goat-climb-dune}

**Marginal tax rates (Ch 12)**  
Federal income tax for 2025 uses *marginal* rates: each rate applies only to income *within* that bracket, not to your whole income. The brackets for **single filers** (tax year 2025) are:

| Rate | Taxable income (single filer) |
|------|-------------------------------|
| 10%  | $0 to $11,925                  |
| 12%  | $11,926 to $48,475             |
| 22%  | $48,476 to $103,350            |
| 24%  | $103,351 to $197,300           |
| 32%  | $197,301 to $250,525           |
| 35%  | $250,526 to $626,350           |
| 37%  | $626,351 and above             |

Source: [Federal income tax rates and brackets (IRS)](https://www.irs.gov/filing/federal-income-tax-rates-and-brackets).

---

1. A single filer has **$50,000** in taxable income in 2025. How is the federal tax on that income calculated?

```{mcq}
#| label: gcd-1-how
1. 22% of $50,000, because 22% is the rate for that bracket.
2. 10% of the first $11,925, plus 12% of the next $36,550, plus 22% only on the amount over $48,475. [correct]
3. The average of 10%, 12%, and 22% applied to $50,000.
4. It depends on whether they take the standard deduction.
```

2. For that same taxpayer with $50,000 taxable income, what is their **marginal** federal tax rate—i.e., the rate that applies to the *next* dollar of taxable income?

```{mcq}
#| label: gcd-2-marg
1. 10%
2. 12%
3. 22% [correct]
4. Some average of 10%, 12%, and 22%.
```

3. They get a raise and their taxable income goes from $50,000 to **$51,000**. How much federal tax do they owe on that *additional* $1,000 of income?

```{mcq}
#| label: gcd-3-extra
1. $220 [correct]
2. $1,100
3. $11,220
4. 22% of their total $51,000 income.
```

4. Because of marginal rates, when your taxable income rises, what happens to the fraction of each *extra* dollar you keep after federal income tax?

```{mcq}
#| label: gcd-4-takehome
1. It stays the same no matter how much you earn.
2. It goes down as you move into higher brackets—each additional dollar is taxed at a higher rate, so you keep less of it. [correct]
3. It goes up, because higher earners get better tax breaks.
4. It depends only on your total income, not on which bracket you're in.
```

5. When you get a raise that pushes you into a higher bracket (e.g., from 22% to 24%), what happens to your **total** after-tax income?

```{mcq}
#| label: gcd-5-always-gain
1. It can go down, because you're now in a higher bracket.
2. It stays the same, because the extra tax cancels out the raise.
3. It always goes up—you never have less after-tax income when you earn more. [correct]
4. It depends on whether you're single or married.
```

6. A raise always increases your after-tax income (you never end up with less). Even so, someone might feel a raise "isn't worth it." **Select all that can be real reasons or trade-offs** that might make a raise feel not worth it to them.

```{mcq}
#| label: gcd-6-effort
1. The trade-off between the extra effort, hours, or stress to earn the raise and the extra take-home pay they'd actually keep. [correct]
2. Losing eligibility for federal benefits (e.g., SNAP, Medicaid) when income rises, so total resources go down even though pay went up. [correct]
3. Concern that higher income could mean a higher chance of IRS audit or scrutiny. [correct]
4. On principle, not wanting to pay more total tax to the government. [correct]
5. The raise would move them into a higher bracket and leave them with less after-tax income than before.
6. The government would tax the raise at 100%, so they'd keep nothing of the extra income.
7. They'd have to work fewer hours, and that wouldn't be worth it.
8. Their employer would cut their base salary by the amount of the raise.
```

7. In one sentence, what does "on the margin" mean in the phrase "marginal tax rate"?

`r devoirs::devoirs_text("gcd-7-sentence")`

:::
<!-- end of exr-goat-climb-dune -->
